title: Null-Aware Operators (??) 🛡️
description: Provide a default backup plan just in case a variable is missing.

slides.title: The Backup Plan 🛡️
slides.content:
# Handling the Unknown

Writing `if` statements to constantly check `if (name != null)` takes up a lot of space.
Dart provides ultra-fast shortcuts to handle null values instantly on a single line!

The **If-Null Operator (`??`)** provides a default "fallback" value if the variable happens to be empty.

slides.title: The Quick Fallback (??) 🪂

quizData.question: What is the primary function of the `??` operator?

options.text: To crash the app if the variable is null.
To provide a default fallback value if the left side evaluates to null.
To check if two variables are equal.

quizData.explanation: The if-null operator (??) safely swaps in default data to prevent your app from crashing.

slides.title: The Safe Call (?.) 📞

quizData.question:
Fill in the exact two characters to safely call a method on a potentially empty user object:
user__.logout();
quizData.explanation: If 'user' is null, the '?.' simply skips the '.logout()' command entirely, preventing a crash.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Combine both operators for incredible one-line power! (If message is null, safely abort `.toUpperCase()` and fallback to 'HIDDEN').
